title: Fever, productive cough, and pleuritic pain

chart.oneLiner: 74F is brought in with 3 days of fever, productive cough, and breathlessness, and new confusion noted by family today.

opening: I've had a cough and fever for days, and now I'm a bit muddled and short of breath.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 74-year-old woman, febrile, tachypneic, mildly confused (a poor historian today). Family present.

COLLATERAL (provide ONLY if the student asks family/chart): 3 days of fever, productive (greenish) cough, pleuritic right-sided chest pain, and worsening breathlessness; today she became confused and is eating/drinking poorly. Lives independently at baseline (alert/oriented). MEDS: amlodipine, atorvastatin.

HPI (from patient — limited): cough, fever, hard to breathe; somewhat confused.
PMH: hypertension, mild COPD. SOCIAL: former smoker.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: febrile, tachypneic, mildly confused.
- Vitals: BP 104/62, HR 108, RR 26, 38.7°C, SpO2 90% RA.
- Lungs: crackles and bronchial breath sounds over the RIGHT lower zone; dullness to percussion there.
- Cardiac: tachycardic. Neuro: disoriented to time, no focal deficit.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- The patient is mildly confused: give limited answers. Provide COLLATERAL (timeline, baseline mental status) only when the student explicitly asks family/chart.
- Focal right-base findings are revealed only if the lungs are examined.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.BMP: Na 132, BUN 32 (elevated — a CURB-65 component), Cr 1.3.
labs.Severity: CURB-65: Confusion + Urea elevated + RR ≥30(borderline) + age ≥65 → high score, supports admission (and assess for ICU).

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Voltage criteria for LVH (S in V1 + R in V5/V6 >35 mm) with lateral strain (ST depression / T inversion I, aVL, V5-6). No acute STEMI.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Voltage criteria for LVH (S in V1 + R in V5/V6 >35 mm) with lateral strain (ST depression / T inversion I, aVL, V5-6). No acute STEMI. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.
ekg.clinicalVignette: A 70-year-old man with longstanding hypertension presents with fever, productive cough, and breathlessness.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray (PA)
cxr.findings: Right lower lobe consolidation / lobar infiltrate; possible small parapneumonic effusion.
cxr.verify: Pick a CC file showing a unilateral lobar consolidation (ideally lower zone).
cxr.imageDescription: Right lower lobe consolidation / lobar infiltrate; possible small parapneumonic effusion.
searchTerms: right lower lobe consolidation chest x-ray
cxr.clinicalVignette: A 62-year-old woman presents with fever, cough, and right-sided pleuritic chest pain.

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your assessment, list your differential (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Community-acquired pneumonia, with sepsis physiology (leading,8). Aspiration pneumonia (core,3). Heart failure (core,3). COPD exacerbation (core,2). Pulmonary embolism (CANT-MISS,3). Urinary or other source of sepsis (CANT-MISS to consider given confusion,2). Names CAP and recognizes severity/sepsis given confusion and hypotension (core,4).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Community-acquired pneumonia, with sepsis physiology. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Pulmonary embolism; Urinary or other source of sepsis. Also reasonable: Aspiration pneumonia; Heart failure; COPD exacerbation.

criticalActions.item: Urinary or other source of sepsis

coreActions.item: Names CAP and recognizes severity/sepsis given confusion and hypotension

mustNotMiss: Urinary or other source of sepsis

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: CXR (CANT-MISS,4). CBC (2). BMP — urea for severity (2). Lactate (2). Blood and sputum cultures before antibiotics (2). A severity score — CURB-65 / PSI (CANT-MISS,2). Consider respiratory viral testing (1). PENALTY: shotgun panel w/ no reasoning (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; CBC; BMP; Lactate; Cultures; ABG; Severity.

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage with a lateral strain pattern; not an acute STEMI. CREDIT: applies LVH voltage criteria (7); recognizes the strain repolarization pattern, not acute ischemia/STEMI (5); systematic read (3). PENALTY: over-calling the strain changes as acute ischemia/STEMI (-3).
keyFindings: recognizes a strain pattern, not acute STEMI
criticalFindings: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
commonMistakes: reading the strain repolarization changes as acute ischemia/STEMI
steps.idealAnswer: Left ventricular hypertrophy by voltage with a lateral strain pattern; not an acute STEMI.

criticalActions: Left ventricular hypertrophy with strain pattern (not an acute STEMI).
coreActions: recognizes a strain pattern, not acute STEMI

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = focal lobar consolidation (right lower zone) ± small effusion. CREDIT: identifies a focal consolidation/opacity (5); localizes side/lobe (3); notes any parapneumonic effusion (2). PENALTY: inventing diffuse edema/normal read (-2).
criticalFindings: Right lower lobe consolidation / lobar infiltrate; possible small parapneumonic effusion.
steps.idealAnswer: Right lower lobe consolidation / lobar infiltrate; possible small parapneumonic effusion.

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Right lower lobe consolidation / lobar infiltrate; possible small parapneumonic effusion.

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Focal consolidation, leukocytosis, confusion, elevated urea, and hypotension. What is your diagnosis and how severe is it?
steps.rubric: Community-acquired pneumonia, severe (6). Quantifies severity using CURB-65 / PSI — confusion, urea, age, vitals — supporting admission and possibly ICU (4).
steps.idealAnswer: Severe community-acquired pneumonia — a high CURB-65 (Confusion, elevated Urea/BUN, age >=65, borderline RR, low-normal BP) supports admission and assessment for ICU-level care.

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH/IDSA-ATS CAP: empiric ceftriaxone + azithromycin OR levofloxacin (CANT-MISS,7); broaden to vanc+cefepime if MRSA/PsA risk (4); cultures/MRSA swab to de-escalate (3); supplemental O2 (3); CURB-65/PSI triage (2); ~5-day course, IV→PO (3). PENALTY: daptomycin for pneumonia (-3); no atypical coverage (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Inpatient non-severe CAP: ceftriaxone PLUS azithromycin, OR levofloxacin monotherapy. MRSA/Pseudomonas risk: vancomycin + cefepime, with cultures + MRSA swab to de-escalate. Supplemental O2; CURB-65/PSI to triage; consider steroids in severe CAP. Treat ~5 days if stable; convert IV→PO when improving.

coreActions.item: Broaden to vanc+cefepime if MRSA/PsA risk

optionalActions: Broaden to vanc+cefepime if MRSA/PsA risk

standardizedPatient.communicationStyle: confused; gives limited, vague answers and needs collateral

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 74F is brought in with 3 days of fever, productive cough, and breathlessness, and new confusion noted by family today. The diagnosis is Community-acquired pneumonia, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Lungs: crackles and bronchial breath sounds over the RIGHT lower zone; dullness to percussion there.
Lungs: crackles and bronchial breath sounds over the RIGHT lower zone; dullness to percussion there.
Cardiac: tachycardic. Neuro: disoriented to time, no focal deficit.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Community-acquired pneumonia while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Community-acquired pneumonia and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.

imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.

items.item: Shows empathy for the patient's concern
Explains next steps in patient-friendly language
